title: smoky roasted hangover cure portuguese white fish gratin
description: a hearty portuguese dish featuring smoky roasted white fish fillets, designed to cure hangovers. flaky fish, crispy potatoes, and a rich smoky flavor make this a comforting meal.
instructions: in a kazan or large cauldron, mix together smoked paprika, chili powder, kosher salt, and black pepper for the spice blend.
add the sliced potatoes to the spice blend and toss to coat evenly.
heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in the kazan over medium heat. add the potatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are golden brown and crispy, about 15 minutes.
remove the potatoes from the kazan and set aside.
add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the kazan. add the white fish fillets and cook until they are opaque and flake easily with a fork, about 5-7 minutes per side.
remove the fish from the kazan and set aside with the potatoes.
add the chicken broth, white wine, and grated lemon zest to the kazan. bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until it has reduced slightly, about 5 minutes.
add the lemon juice and chopped parsley to the kazan. stir to combine.
place the cooked fish and potatoes in a baking dish. pour the sauce from the kazan over the top.
cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 5-10 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
